H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: H-OS-L1 (Pebble, n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, strong confidence) is the largest sample in the dataset with strong normal distribution skewed toward higher scores (66% positive). Site source attribution highest here (26/95) suggesting referral pattern. YouTube shows 30 views — highest reach in dataset. AutoGluon cross-bucket predicts 6.859 for overstim zone, slightly below observed mean.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Pebble is the strongest overstimulation protocol with the best data — most feel noticeable calming within about 10 minutes.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: H-OS-L2 (Rainfall, n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, strong confidence) maintains strong tier with tighter variance showing more consistent outcomes. Qualitative data (n=21) shows fastest settle times in overstim cluster (3/21 immediate). More neutral music opinion than L1. YouTube at 5 views limits reach signal.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Rainfall reliably helps with overstimulation; some listeners feel the effect almost immediately while others take about 10 minutes.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: H-OS-L3 (Glacier, n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, meaningful confidence) recovers toward L1 performance with lowest std in overstim cluster. Embedding drift at 0.818 flagged notable — analytical framing shifted between runs. Qualitative shows balanced music/rhythm opinion split. All data from VOL001 only.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Glacier shows strong effectiveness for overstimulation with balanced, calming rhythm opinions from most listeners.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: H-AX-L1 (Meadow, n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, strong confidence) is the strongest anxiety DESCENT protocol with distribution peaking at scores 8-9 (52%). Qualitative data (n=23) shows moderate-to-noticeable improvement dominant with 10-minute settle time most common. YouTube shows 7 views — low reach. AutoGluon predicts 7.838 for anxiety zone, suggesting potential upside from observed 7.22 mean.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Meadow works well for anxiety relief, especially in therapeutic settings — most listeners settle within 10 minutes and find the rhythm very calming.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: H-AX-L2 (Cottage, n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, meaningful confidence) shows slightly lower mean than L1 but with tighter variance indicating more consistent outcomes. Distribution peaks at 7-8. Qualitative responses (n=20) show variable settle times (5-20 minutes) with more neutral music opinions than L1. YouTube at 6 views limits reach interpretation.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Cottage reliably reduces anxiety across listener types. The music gets generally positive feedback though some find it middle-of-the-road.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: H-AX-L3 (Valley, n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, meaningful confidence) sits between L1 and L2 in the anxiety cluster. Qualitative data (n=20) shows strong 'very settling/calming' rhythm opinions (10/20). All responses from unknown source with YouTube at 4 views. Distribution shows moderate spread consistent with its entry tier.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Valley shows strong potential for both anxiety and mood improvement. The rhythm is especially good at creating a settling, calming effect.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: H-CR-L1 (Anchor, n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, meaningful confidence) performs as structurally expected for crisis protocols where listeners arrive in acute distress. Distribution peaks at 7-8 but with more low scores (5-6) than anxiety protocols. Qualitative data shows 8/24 rated 'significant improvement' — notable given absolute score level. YouTube at 6 views.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Anchor provides good support in immediate crisis situations — many report significant improvement despite arriving in acute distress states.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: H-CR-L2 (Harbor, n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, meaningful confidence) slightly elevates from L1 with similar variance. Faster settle times emerging (5-10 min dominant). Music opinion more divided (neutral 6/16 vs loved 5/16). AutoGluon predicts 7.955 for crisis zone — notable delta from observed 6.52 suggests optimization potential. YouTube at 4 views.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Harbor works effectively in acute distress with a balanced, comfortable rhythm. Settlement times vary from 5-10 minutes for most.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: H-CR-L3 (Horizon, n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, meaningful confidence) shows identical mean to L2 but with wider spread indicating more variable listener experiences. Embedding drift at 0.819 flagged as notable between runs. Qualitative data shows fastest settle times in crisis cluster (7/19 immediate/within 2 min). Highest 'not liked' music opinion in crisis group (4/19). YouTube at 5 views.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Horizon works well even in extreme states — some listeners settle immediately while others take longer. The rhythm feels balanced to most.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: H-SL-L1 (Lantern, n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, strong confidence) performs above 7.0 mean consistent with DESCENT design intent for sleep. Distribution shows strong upper tail (9-10 = 16/54). Qualitative (n=15) indicates fast settle (5 min dominant). AutoGluon predicts 7.539 for sleep zone, aligning with observed mean. Embedding drift at 0.719 flagged notable. YouTube at 4 views.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Lantern works well for sleep — most people settle within 5 minutes and find it creates good conditions for rest.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: H-SL-L2 (Hammock, n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, meaningful confidence) shows highest mean in sleep series with tighter variance. Distribution skews toward higher scores. Qualitative (n=15) shows 10-minute settle dominant. Music opinion mixed (neutral 6 vs liked 5) — slightly less favorable than L1. YouTube at 5 views.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Hammock supports sleep effectively with most finding it helps them wind down, though some report longer settle times.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: H-SL-L3 (Midnight, n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, meaningful confidence) performs comparably to L1/L2 in sleep cluster. Qualitative (n=22) shows 5-minute settle dominant (8/22). Activity context includes 'trying to sleep' (8/22) and 'working' (4/22). YouTube at 3 views — lowest in series.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Midnight shows good potential for sleep with 5-minute settle times common. Works well for both relaxation and active listening contexts.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: T-FC-L1 (Compass, n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, meaningful confidence) shows bimodal distribution with peaks at 0 and 8 — warrants monitoring at higher volume. Qualitative (n=23) shows 'noticeable improvement' dominant (10/23) but 'slightly busy' rhythm opinion (9/23) indicates potential groove mismatch for some listeners. YouTube at 5 views.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Compass helps with focus but shows mixed responses — some love it while others find it slightly too active for their needs.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: T-FC-L2 (Waypoint, n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.6, meaningful confidence) shows strong performance with distribution spike at 9 (10/32). Qualitative (n=17) shows 10-minute settle dominant (9/17). Mixed music opinion (neutral 6 vs loved 5). YouTube at 4 views.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Waypoint effective for focus and clarity with noticeable improvement. The rhythm is especially well-liked by most listeners.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: T-FC-L3 (Summit, n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, meaningful confidence) is the top performer in THRIVING with 71% positive responses and 10/35 at score 10. Qualitative 'significant improvement' dominant (11/29). AutoGluon predicts 7.728, confirming high efficacy. YouTube at 15 views — strong reach for meaningful confidence tier.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Summit is the highest-rated protocol overall — excellent for focus support with significant improvement reported by many.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: T-CF-L1 (Ember, n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, meaningful confidence) shows moderate improvement ratings with slight improvement dominant (8/21). Qualitative settle times evenly split between immediate and 10-minute. Music 'liked' (9/21) and 'loved' (6/21) positive. YouTube at 2 views — very low reach.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Ember helps with creative flow — listeners report slight to moderate improvement, with the music generally liked or loved.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: T-CF-L2 (Current, n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, meaningful confidence) shows tightest variance in creative flow with balanced distribution. Qualitative 'loved it' and 'liked it' tied (6 each). Settle times 10 minutes (5/15) and 5 minutes (4/15) dominant. YouTube at 2 views.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Current supports creative work well with strong positive ratings. Most find the rhythm balanced and comfortable.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: T-CF-L3 (Aurora, n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, developing confidence) shows strongest performance in creative flow with lowest variance despite developing tier. Distribution shows strong positive cluster (7-9). Qualitative (n=25) shows balanced/comfortable rhythm dominant (14/25). YouTube at 7 views — moderate reach for developing protocol.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Aurora is the strongest creative flow protocol — listeners love the balanced rhythm and many report significant improvement.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: T-MB-L1 (Stone, n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, meaningful confidence) shows tightest variance in maintenance series. Qualitative 'balanced/comfortable' rhythm dominant (12/23). Settle times spread (20 min 9/23, 5 min 6/23). Notable cross-run drift (sim=0.565). YouTube at 3 views.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Stone works well for maintenance with the rhythm creating a comfortable state. Settlement times vary across listeners.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: T-MB-L2 (Axis, n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, meaningful confidence) shows higher variance despite mid-range mean. Distribution shows spike at 8 (7/22). Qualitative balanced/comfortable and slightly busy tied (7 each). Notable drift (sim=0.637). YouTube at 4 views.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Axis supports sustained balance with mixed rhythm opinions — some find it balanced, others slightly busy.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: T-MB-L3 (Orbit, n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, developing confidence) shows developing signal with highest mean in maintenance series despite low n. Qualitative 'noticeable improvement' dominant (6/19). Has highest drift in dataset (sim=0.557) — interpret with caution. YouTube at 3 views.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Orbit is effective for maintaining balance state with noticeable improvement. The rhythm is consistently well-liked.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: X-ME-L1 (Breeze, n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, meaningful confidence) appears frequently in AutoGluon cross-bucket predictions for other zones, suggesting broad efficacy. Qualitative 'loved it' highest in mood/energy series (10/22). 20-minute settle dominant (6/22) — longer integration time. YouTube at 8 views.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Breeze is excellent for mood and energy with highest 'loved it' ratings. Takes longer (20 min) to fully integrate for some.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: X-ME-L2 (Sunrise, n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, meaningful confidence) shows strong qualitative ratings with 'loved it' (6/15) and 'balanced/comfortable' rhythm (7/15). Significant improvement reported (7/15). 20-minute settle tied with immediate (4 each) — variable integration. YouTube at 8 views.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Sunrise good for improving mood and energy with strong loved it ratings. Works well for sleep and therapy contexts.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: X-ME-L3 (Updraft, n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, developing confidence) shows lowest mean in mood/energy series but strong 'loved it' music (7/25). 5-minute settle dominant (8/25) — faster than L1/L2. No 'made things worse' reports despite lower mean. YouTube at 4 views.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Updraft supports mood with balanced opinions. Faster settle times (5 min) than other mood protocols despite similar effectiveness.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: X-MD-L1 (Footpath, n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, meaningful confidence) shows widest variance in motivation series with 45% positive. Qualitative settle shows 5-minute dominant (9/17). Music 'liked' (8/17) vs neutral (5/17). Notable drift (sim=0.830). YouTube at 5 views.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Footpath helps with motivation, especially for studying and relaxation contexts. Most find the rhythm comfortable.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: X-MD-L2 (Trail, n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, developing confidence) shows lowest mean in motivation series. Qualitative 'loved it' (9/21) strong despite moderate outcome score — suggests disconnect between experience and reported outcome. Settle times longer (20 min 5/21). YouTube at 7 views.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Trail good for increasing motivation despite moderate outcome scores — listeners love the music even when outcomes vary.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: X-MD-L3 (Switchback, n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, developing confidence) shows highest mean in motivation despite high variance. Qualitative 'moderate improvement' dominant (8/17). Settle times show 5-minute (5/17) and immediate (4/17) — faster than L1/L2. Notable drift (sim=0.801). YouTube at 7 views.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Switchback effectively boosts motivation with faster settle times than other motivation protocols. Shows promise for mood benefits too.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: X-MC-L1 (Cavern, n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, meaningful confidence) shows balanced distribution with qualitative 'loved it' and 'liked it' tied (9 each). Settle times split evenly between 5-10 min. Music/opinion data shows strong positive response despite moderate mean. YouTube at 8 views.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Cavern supports mind clarity with many reporting improvement. The music gets high praise from both users and clinicians.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: X-MC-L2 (Starlight, n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, developing confidence) shows developing signal with highest qualitative ratings in mind clarity series. 'Very settling/calming' rhythm dominant (12/20). Settle times show immediate (6) and 10 min (6) tied. YouTube at 12 views — best reach in TRANSFORMING.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Starlight good for clarity and focus with strong 'very settling' rhythm opinions. Settlement happens quickly for many.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: X-MC-L3 (Cosmos, n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45, developing confidence) shows concerning pattern: lowest mean in dataset with widest spread and bimodal distribution. 10% (1/10) at score 0, 20% reported it made things worse (2/10 in qualitative). 'Too busy' rhythm opinion (4/17) indicates groove mismatch for some. AutoGluon cross-bucket recommends X-ME-L1/L2 — warrants population investigation at higher n.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Cosmos shows mixed results — some love the clarity, others find it too busy. May work better for specific subgroups.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The Drum Protocols demonstrate solid cross-series efficacy with all means clustering 6.5-7.0, though variance patterns distinguish the three design arcs.
• HEALING leads in data volume (n=574) and consistency (std=1.77), with overstimulation and sleep protocols showing strongest signal
• THRIVING records the highest top-end scores, particularly T-FC-L3 (mean=7.63, 71% positive) and maintains tighter variance than TRANSFORMING
• TRANSFORMING carries lower mean (6.59) and widest spread (std=1.87) — structurally expected given its ascending-arc design for low-affect states
• All three series remain in meaningful signal territory; no series shows failure-mode distributions
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
The three series exhibit profiles that align with their design intent.
• HEALING demonstrates strong reliability for nervous system regulation, particularly in anxiety and over-stimulation entry points where DESCENT protocols excel
• THRIVING shows the highest mean scores and most homogeneous response profiles, with Focus & Clarity protocols standing out as top performers
• TRANSFORMING exhibits higher variance — this reflects both its challenging user population and the longer time horizon required for ascending-arc outcomes
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Notable anomalies warrant investigation rather than immediate concern.
• X-MC-L3 (n=10, mean=5.7, std=2.45) shows bimodal distribution with 20% reporting negative effects — potential population mismatch requiring subgroup analysis at higher n
• AutoGluon cross-bucket routing frequently recommends X-ME-L1/L2 for other zones, suggesting broad efficacy or potential boundary-blurring that warrants investigation
• Multiple THRIVING protocols (T-MB-L3, T-MB-L1, T-CF-L3) show significant analytical drift between runs — interpret their readings with appropriate caution
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
The data supports several concrete refinements:
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3 (n=10) — investigate population split or subgroup effects due to bimodal distribution; consider creating L2 variant with adjusted entrainment curves
• T-FC-L3 (n=35, mean=7.63) — strong performer with high AutoGluon prediction (7.728); create L2 variant building on its validated efficacy
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] AutoGluon cross-bucket routing recommends X-ME-L1/X-ME-L2 for crisis and low-affect states — consider adjusting protocol routing logic to leverage this boundary-crossing signal
• H-OS-L1 (n=95) — highest performing overstimulation protocol; adjust adviser routing to prioritize for listeners in acute overstimulation states
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding context reveals analytical drift and semantic clustering.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: Multiple THRIVING protocols (T-MB-L3 sim=0.557, T-MB-L1 sim=0.565, T-CF-L3 sim=0.586) show significant drift — interpret their analytical readings with caution as framing has substantially shifted between runs
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: T-MB-L2/T-MB-L3 cluster tightly (sim=0.844), suggesting shared efficacy mechanisms; T-CF-L3 appears analytically distinct (mean_sim=0.742)
• Confidence in embedding analysis is moderate due to limited run history; protocols with high drift warrant close longitudinal monitoring

by_protocol.X-ME-L1: X-ME-L1 (Breeze, n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, meaningful confidence) appears frequently in AutoGluon cross-bucket predictions for other zones, suggesting broad efficacy. Qualitative 'loved it' highest in mood/energy series (10/22). 20-minute settle dominant (6/22) — longer integration time. YouTube at 8 views.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: X-ME-L2 (Sunrise, n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, meaningful confidence) shows strong qualitative ratings with 'loved it' (6/15) and 'balanced/comfortable' rhythm (7/15). Significant improvement reported (7/15). 20-minute settle tied with immediate (4 each) — variable integration. YouTube at 8 views.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: X-ME-L3 (Updraft, n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, developing confidence) shows lowest mean in mood/energy series but strong 'loved it' music (7/25). 5-minute settle dominant (8/25) — faster than L1/L2. No 'made things worse' reports despite lower mean. YouTube at 4 views.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Meadow works well for anxiety relief, especially in therapeutic settings — most listeners settle within 10 minutes and find the rhythm very calming.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Cottage reliably reduces anxiety across listener types. The music gets generally positive feedback though some find it middle-of-the-road.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Valley shows strong potential for both anxiety and mood improvement. The rhythm is especially good at creating a settling, calming effect.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Anchor provides good support in immediate crisis situations — many report significant improvement despite arriving in acute distress states.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Harbor works effectively in acute distress with a balanced, comfortable rhythm. Settlement times vary from 5-10 minutes for most.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Horizon works well even in extreme states — some listeners settle immediately while others take longer. The rhythm feels balanced to most.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Pebble is the strongest overstimulation protocol with the best data — most feel noticeable calming within about 10 minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Rainfall reliably helps with overstimulation; some listeners feel the effect almost immediately while others take about 10 minutes.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Glacier shows strong effectiveness for overstimulation with balanced, calming rhythm opinions from most listeners.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Lantern works well for sleep — most people settle within 5 minutes and find it creates good conditions for rest.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Hammock supports sleep effectively with most finding it helps them wind down, though some report longer settle times.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Midnight shows good potential for sleep with 5-minute settle times common. Works well for both relaxation and active listening contexts.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Ember helps with creative flow — listeners report slight to moderate improvement, with the music generally liked or loved.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Current supports creative work well with strong positive ratings. Most find the rhythm balanced and comfortable.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Aurora is the strongest creative flow protocol — listeners love the balanced rhythm and many report significant improvement.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Compass helps with focus but shows mixed responses — some love it while others find it slightly too active for their needs.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Waypoint effective for focus and clarity with noticeable improvement. The rhythm is especially well-liked by most listeners.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Summit is the highest-rated protocol overall — excellent for focus support with significant improvement reported by many.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Stone works well for maintenance with the rhythm creating a comfortable state. Settlement times vary across listeners.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Axis supports sustained balance with mixed rhythm opinions — some find it balanced, others slightly busy.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Orbit is effective for maintaining balance state with noticeable improvement. The rhythm is consistently well-liked.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Cavern supports mind clarity with many reporting improvement. The music gets high praise from both users and clinicians.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Starlight good for clarity and focus with strong 'very settling' rhythm opinions. Settlement happens quickly for many.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Cosmos shows mixed results — some love the clarity, others find it too busy. May work better for specific subgroups.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Footpath helps with motivation, especially for studying and relaxation contexts. Most find the rhythm comfortable.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Trail good for increasing motivation despite moderate outcome scores — listeners love the music even when outcomes vary.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Switchback effectively boosts motivation with faster settle times than other motivation protocols. Shows promise for mood benefits too.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Breeze is excellent for mood and energy with highest 'loved it' ratings. Takes longer (20 min) to fully integrate for some.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Sunrise good for improving mood and energy with strong loved it ratings. Works well for sleep and therapy contexts.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Updraft supports mood with balanced opinions. Faster settle times (5 min) than other mood protocols despite similar effectiveness.
